decision_question: Should we invest in or prepare for nuclear fusion as a commercially viable energy source within the next 15 years?

hypotheses.hypothesis: Nuclear fusion will not achieve grid-connected electricity generation before 2040.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: If true, near-term investment in fusion is premature; focus on other low-carbon technologies.
needed_evidence: Published timelines from ITER, SPARC, and other major projects; historical delays in fusion research.

hypotheses.hypothesis: The LCOE of first-generation fusion plants will be higher than solar+storage by 2035.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: If true, fusion will struggle to compete without carbon pricing or subsidies.
needed_evidence: LCOE projections from fusion companies and independent analysts; solar+storage cost trends.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Private fusion funding has shifted from early-stage to later-stage rounds, indicating maturation.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: If true, fusion is attracting serious capital; if false, it remains speculative.
needed_evidence: Funding data by round size and stage; number of startups reaching Series C or beyond.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Tritium supply constraints will limit fusion deployment to <10 GW by 2050.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: If true, fusion scale-up is bottlenecked; alternative breeding strategies needed.
needed_evidence: Tritium production capacity from CANDU reactors and other sources; fusion reactor tritium consumption rates.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Regulatory frameworks for fusion will be less stringent than fission, reducing licensing time and cost.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: If true, fusion may have a deployment advantage over advanced fission.
needed_evidence: Comparison of regulatory approaches (e.g., US NRC, UK EA); licensing timelines for fusion vs. fission.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Industrial heat applications (e.g., steel, cement) will be the first commercial fusion market, not electricity.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: If true, fusion companies should target industrial customers; grid electricity may be secondary.
needed_evidence: Company announcements of industrial partnerships; heat demand and temperature requirements.

hypotheses.hypothesis: China will lead fusion commercialization due to state-backed projects and supply chain control.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: If true, Western investors may face competitive disadvantage; policy implications.
needed_evidence: China fusion budget and milestones; rare earth magnet supply chain; CFETR timeline.

market_sizing_plan.sizing_question: How large is the credible opportunity for Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications, and which variables still need validation?

methods.formula: Total addressable market (TWh) = Global electricity demand (TWh) * % addressable by fusion (based on grid parity and baseload needs)
known_limitations: Assumes fusion achieves cost parity; ignores regional variations

methods.formula: Market size (GW) = Number of fusion plants * average capacity per plant (GW)
variables: Number of plants by 2050 (based on announced projects and realistic build rates)
Average plant capacity (e.g., 500 MW for ARC-class)
known_limitations: Highly speculative; depends on technology success and regulatory approval

methods.formula: Adopted capacity (GW) = (Number of potential customers) * (adoption rate) * (average capacity per customer)
variables: Potential customers: utilities, industrial heat users, data centers
Adoption rate based on cost competitiveness and risk appetite
Capacity per customer (e.g., 100 MW for data center)
preferred_sources: Data center energy consumption reports (IEA, Uptime Institute)
known_limitations: Adoption rate is highly uncertain; early adopters may be niche

methods.formula: Value pool ($) = (Electricity revenue) + (heat revenue) + (hydrogen production revenue) + (other byproducts)
known_limitations: Byproduct markets are nascent; revenue stacking uncertain

methods.formula: Maximum deployable capacity (GW) = (Tritium supply) / (tritium consumption per GW-year) * (breeding ratio factor)
variables: Tritium production from CANDU and other sources
known_limitations: Tritium breeding not yet demonstrated; supply constraints may be binding

market_sizing_plan.evidence_rule: Use public source values when available; keep missing variables as validation tasks.

validation_data_needs.metric: Levelized cost of electricity for fusion vs. solar+storage vs. fission (USD/MWh)
Number of fusion startups and their funding stages (seed, Series A, B, C+)
Tritium production capacity from existing sources (kg/year)
Industrial heat demand by temperature range (PJ/year)
Data center energy consumption growth rate (% per year)
Government fusion R&D budgets by country (USD/year)
Market share of leading fusion companies (by funding, patents, or partnerships)
Timeline milestones from ITER, SPARC, CFETR, and DEMO
Historical cost overruns and delays in large fusion projects

chart_data_needs.title: Fusion LCOE vs. Solar+Storage and Fission (2025-2050)
required_metrics: Solar+storage LCOE projections (USD/MWh) for same years
Fission LCOE projections (USD/MWh) for same years
preferred_sources: Lazard Levelized Cost of Energy Analysis
Company roadmaps (Commonwealth Fusion Systems, TAE Technologies)
search_queries: IEA World Energy Outlook 2024 LCOE nuclear
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use public projections from authoritative sources; if fusion LCOE is not available, note as 'not yet published' and use company targets with uncertainty bands.

chart_data_needs.title: Fusion Funding by Stage (2015-2024)
chart_data_needs.executive_question: Is fusion investment maturing or still speculative?
required_metrics: Annual venture capital funding ($M) for fusion startups, broken down by seed, Series A, Series B, Series C+
Number of fusion startups reaching Series C or beyond
search_queries: fusion energy venture capital funding 2024 PitchBook
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Cross-reference PitchBook and Crunchbase; if data is behind paywall, use Fusion Industry Association public summaries.

chart_data_needs.title: Tritium Supply vs. Demand for Fusion Fleet (2030-2050)
chart_data_needs.executive_question: Can tritium supply support significant fusion capacity?
required_metrics: Global tritium production capacity (kg/year) from CANDU reactors and other sources, 2025-2050
Projected tritium demand (kg/year) for fusion fleet scenarios (e.g., 1 GW, 10 GW, 50 GW installed capacity)
Tritium consumption rate per fusion plant (kg/GW-year)
comparison_set: Supply vs. demand under different deployment scenarios
preferred_sources: Fusion engineering papers (e.g., Tritium Breeding Ratio requirements)
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use publicly available IAEA data; if CANDU production is proprietary, use estimates from academic literature. Note uncertainty ranges.

chart_data_needs.title: Timeline Comparison: ITER, SPARC, CFETR, DEMO
chart_data_needs.executive_question: What are the realistic milestones for first fusion power?
required_metrics: Key milestones and target dates for ITER (first plasma, DT operation, full power)
Key milestones for SPARC (construction start, first plasma, Q>1 demonstration)
Key milestones for CFETR (design completion, construction start, operation)
Key milestones for DEMO (conceptual design, engineering design, construction)
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use official project announcements; if dates are delayed, note the most recent update. Include historical delays as annotations.

chart_data_needs.title: Regulatory Licensing Timeline: Fusion vs. Fission
chart_data_needs.executive_question: Does fusion have a regulatory advantage?
required_metrics: Average licensing timeline (months) for fusion reactors (e.g., from application to approval) in US, UK, Canada
Average licensing timeline (months) for fission reactors (e.g., small modular reactors) in same countries
comparison_set: Fusion vs. fission licensing duration by country
preferred_sources: US Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) fusion regulatory framework documents
UK Environment Agency fusion licensing guidance
Canadian Nuclear Safety Commission (CNSC) pre-licensing reviews
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use official regulatory documents; if fusion licensing timelines are not yet established, note as 'in development' and use fission timelines for comparison.

chart_data_needs.title: Industrial Heat Demand by Temperature Range (PJ/year)
chart_data_needs.executive_question: Which industrial sectors could be early fusion adopters?
required_metrics: Industrial heat demand (PJ/year) by temperature range: low (<150°C), medium (150-400°C), high (400-800°C), very high (>800°C)
Sector breakdown: steel, cement, chemicals, refining, etc.
comparison_set: Heat demand by temperature and sector
search_queries: IEA industrial heat demand by temperature 2024
US DOE industrial heat decarbonization roadmap
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use IEA or DOE public datasets; if temperature breakdown is not available, use sector-level data and estimate temperature ranges.

chart_data_needs.title: Government Fusion R&D Budgets by Country (USD/year)
chart_data_needs.executive_question: Which countries are investing most in fusion?
required_metrics: Annual government fusion R&D budget (USD) for US, EU (including Euratom), China, Japan, South Korea, UK, Russia, India
Breakdown by program (e.g., ITER contribution, domestic programs)
preferred_sources: US DOE Fusion Energy Sciences budget documents
China Ministry of Science and Technology announcements
Japan Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT)
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use official government budget documents; if China budget is not publicly available, use estimates from academic papers or state media. Convert all to USD using average exchange rates.

storyline_plan.core_question: Should we invest in or prepare for nuclear fusion as a commercially viable energy source within the next 15 years?
storyline_plan.central_thesis: The storyline should be led by the strongest public evidence, then translated into management choices.
storyline_plan.narrative_focus: Evidence is densest around: funding, timeline, adoption, technology
storyline_plan.structure_logic: Open with the executive decision, show source-backed data exhibits, then move through business implications, risks and action gates.
evidence_must_cover: To date, Milestone awardees have collectively raised over $350 million of new private funding since their selection into the program was announced in May 2023, compared to the $46 million of federal funding initially committed for negotiated milestones.

To date, Milestone awardees have collectively raised over $350 million of new private funding since their selection into the program was announced in May 2023, compared to the $46 million of federal funding initially committed for negotiated milestones.

Highlights of the FY 2027 Request The FES FY 2027 Request of $755.3 million is a decrease of $50.406 million below FY 2026 Enacted, with reduced funding for core research to offset increases for high priority research activities and facility operations.

Highlights of the FY 2027 Request The FES FY 2027 Request of $755.3 million is a decrease of $50.406 million below FY 2026 Enacted, with reduced funding for core research to offset increases for high priority research activities and facility operations.

All 8 awardees are presently working toward presenting pre-conceptual designs and technology roadmaps of their FPP concepts within the first 18 months of the Milestone program—roughly late 2025 (18 months into the Milestone Program).

Department of Energy (DOE) today announced $107 million in funding for six projects in the Fusion Innovative Research Engine (FIRE) Collaboratives , and that several privately funded fusion companies have completed early critical-path science and technology (S&T) milestones in the Milestone-Based Fusion Development Program (“the Milestone Program”).

storyline_plan.charting_rule: Charts may use only evidence_ledger values, dates, source counts or same-unit comparable values tied to public URLs.
